items.q: Why do text types have predictable features?
items.correct: Conventions develop because they serve communicative purpose — a recipe needs imperatives because it instructs; a news report needs the inverted pyramid because of how readers scan.
items.misconception: Text types have fixed features just because tradition says so.
items.random_wrong: Text types are arbitrary and have no patterns.
items.why: Conventions are functional, not arbitrary. Students treat genre features as rules to memorise; understanding their purpose explains why they exist and when they can be broken.

items.q: What is overextension in child language?
items.correct: When a child applies a word more broadly than adults do — e.g. calling all four-legged animals 'dog' before learning distinct categories.
items.misconception: Overextension is when children use very long words they don't understand.
items.random_wrong: Overextension is when children speak too loudly.
items.why: Overextension is about semantic categories, not word length or volume. It shows children are forming hypotheses about meaning, then refining them — a key window into acquisition.

items.correct: A syntactic unit headed by a noun, which may include determiners, adjectives and modifiers — e.g. 'the big red car' is a noun phrase functioning as a single grammatical unit.
items.misconception: A noun phrase is just a noun by itself.
items.random_wrong: A noun phrase is a sentence that contains nouns.
items.why: Noun phrases can be one word ('cats') or many ('the cats that lived next door'). The phrase functions as a unit — subject, object or complement. Students reduce NP to a single noun, missing constituent structure.

items.q: What does the L in PEEL stand for?
items.why: Correct answer: Link. “List” sounds right because it borrows the language of the topic — but it fails this question. Re-read the notes on why.

items.q: Which reading skill hunts specific facts?
items.why: The answer examiners want is: Scanning. “Skimming” is the distractor this question was built around — if it tempted you, that's the exact gap to close.

items.q: How many letters in PEEL paragraph structure?
items.why: The real figure is 4 letters. The wrong options cluster deliberately close — anchor the exact number to one concrete fact so it can't drift.
